Tomato_Bacterial_Spot.symptom: Small, water-soaked spots that turn dark and necrotic. Spots may have a yellow halo and eventually cause leaf drop.
Tomato_Bacterial_Spot.organic: Apply copper-based sprays. Remove and destroy infected plant debris.
Tomato_Bacterial_Spot.chemical: Use bactericides containing Streptomycin or copper hydroxide.

Tomato_Early_Blight.symptom: Brown circular spots with concentric rings, often called 'target' spots. Older leaves are affected first.
Tomato_Early_Blight.organic: Mulch around plants to prevent soil splash. Prune lower leaves. Use Neem oil.
Tomato_Early_Blight.chemical: Apply fungicides containing Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb.

Tomato_Late_Blight.symptom: Dark irregular wet lesions with rapid spread. In humid conditions, white mold may appear on the underside.
Tomato_Late_Blight.organic: Destroy infected plants immediately. Avoid overhead watering. Use copper soap.
Tomato_Late_Blight.chemical: High-strength fungicides like Ridomil Gold or Revus are required immediately.

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.symptom: Pale green or yellow spots on the upper leaf surface, with olive-green to brown velvety mold on the underside.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.organic: Increase air circulation. Reduce humidity below 85%. Use baking soda spray.

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.symptom: Small, circular spots with dark brown margins and tan or gray centers. Black specks may be visible in centers.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.organic: Keep foliage dry. Remove infected lower leaves. Use compost tea.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.chemical: Apply Chlorothalonil or copper-based fungicides every 7-10 days.

Tomato_Spider_Mites.symptom: Yellow or white stippling on leaves. Fine webbing may be visible on the leaf underside.
Tomato_Spider_Mites.organic: Blast with high-pressure water. Introduce predatory mites. Use Insecticidal soap.
Tomato_Spider_Mites.chemical: Use miticides like Abamectin or Bifenthrin.

Tomato_Target_Spot.symptom: Small, circular to irregular brown spots with a yellow halo. Spots often develop a target-like appearance.
Tomato_Target_Spot.organic: Space plants for airflow. Remove lower leaves. Use copper fungicides.
Tomato_Target_Spot.chemical: Apply fungicides containing Azoxystrobin or Pyraclostrobin.

Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptom: Stunted growth, yellowing along leaf margins, and characteristic upward leaf curling.
Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.organic: Control Whiteflies with yellow sticky traps. Use reflective mulches.
Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.chemical: No chemical cure for virus. Use insecticides to kill Whiteflies (vectors).

Tomato_Mosaic_Virus.symptom: Mottling or mosaic patterns of light and dark green on leaves, often with leaf curling.
Tomato_Mosaic_Virus.organic: Remove infected plants. Wash hands with milk/soap after handling. Use resistant varieties.
Tomato_Mosaic_Virus.chemical: No chemical treatment. Focus on sanitation and controlling aphids.

Tomato_Healthy.symptom: Green leaf with no visible disease, consistent color and smooth texture.
Tomato_Healthy.organic: Keep doing what you're doing! Use compost for nutrients.
Tomato_Healthy.chemical: No chemicals needed. Maintain regular fertilization.

Potato_Early_Blight.symptom: Target-like dark brown spots with concentric rings. Usually appears on older leaves first.
Potato_Early_Blight.organic: Rotate crops every 3 years. Use mulch. Apply Neem oil early.
Potato_Early_Blight.chemical: Use fungicides containing Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb.

Potato_Late_Blight.symptom: Large water-soaked dark brown or black spots. White fungal growth may develop on the bottom.
Potato_Late_Blight.organic: Plant resistant varieties. Destroy volunteer potatoes. Use copper sprays.
Potato_Late_Blight.chemical: Apply preventative fungicides like Chlorothalonil before rain.

Potato_Healthy.symptom: Healthy green potato leaf with smooth surface and no discoloration.
Potato_Healthy.organic: Maintain soil moisture. Use organic fertilizer.

Corn_Common_Rust.symptom: Small, reddish-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces. Release dusty spores.
Corn_Common_Rust.organic: Plant resistant hybrids. Early planting. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Corn_Common_Rust.chemical: Apply fungicides like Tilt or Headline if infection starts early.

Corn_Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptom: Long, cigar-shaped grayish-green or tan lesions that run parallel to the leaf veins.
Corn_Northern_Leaf_Blight.organic: Tillage to bury infected debris. Crop rotation. Resistant varieties.
Corn_Northern_Leaf_Blight.chemical: Apply fungicides like Quilt or Stratego during silking stage.

Corn_Gray_Leaf_Spot.symptom: Rectangular, gray-to-tan lesions with straight edges parallel to veins.
Corn_Gray_Leaf_Spot.organic: Increase plant spacing. Rotate with non-host crops (soybeans).
Corn_Gray_Leaf_Spot.chemical: Use fungicides containing Strobilurin or Triazole.

Corn_Healthy.symptom: Green maize leaf with no visible lesions, spots, or galls.
